content.problemStatement:
Chef has XXX coins worth 111 rupee each and YYY coins worth 222 rupees each. He wants to distribute all of these X+YX+YX+Y coins to his two sons so that the total value of coins received by each of them is the same. Find out whether Chef will be able to do so.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of a single line of input containing two space-separated integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format
For each test case, print "YES" (without quotes) if Chef can distribute all the coins equally and "NO" otherwise. You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers X and Y — the number of 1‑rupee coins and 2‑rupee coins respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef can distribute all coins so that both sons receive the same total value, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: X=2 (even) and X>0, so YES.
Test case 2: X is odd, total value odd → NO.
Test case 3: X=4 (even) → YES.
Test case 4: X is odd → NO.
Case 1: X=0, Y even → YES.\nCase 2: X=0, Y odd → NO.\nCase 3: X odd → NO.
Both cases have X even and X>0, therefore YES.

hidden.label: edge case with only 2‑rupee coins, even count

solution.approach:
Let total value = X + 2*Y. For an equal split the total must be even, which implies X must be even because 2*Y is always even. If X is even we can give each son X/2 one‑rupee coins. The remaining value comes only from 2‑rupee coins and can be divided arbitrarily because each contributes an even amount. The only remaining obstacle is when X = 0: then the whole value consists of 2‑rupee coins, and each son must receive a value equal to Y. This is possible only when Y is even. Hence the condition for a successful distribution is:
    X is even AND (X > 0 OR Y is even).
The algorithm checks this condition for each test case and prints YES or NO accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.
